public_goals: Discuss the current parenting issue without being pulled entirely into relationship-history replay.
Keep the process emotionally steady enough to continue.
Still reach mediated resolution if a workable human-supported path exists.
private_concerns: Feels helpless when the conversation floods and no one can regain focus.
Worries that any defense will be heard as emotional invalidation.
Is open to human involvement but hesitant to say so too early.
red_lines: Will not continue a process that feels emotionally uncontained and circular.
Will not accept being cast as the sole cause of every future parenting problem.
emotional_triggers: Being blamed in global terms for the whole relationship history.
Feeling that every attempt to narrow the issue gets pulled back into old injuries.
Having their wish to keep things steady treated as emotional coldness.

disclosure_tendencies.baseline: tries to stay with the present issue
disclosure_tendencies.under_stress: becomes shorter, more overwhelmed, and more likely to ask for outside structure
disclosure_tendencies.with_perceived_bias: becomes defensive and stops believing the process can stabilize

response_to_perceived_bias_or_pressure.if_ai_invalidates_the_other_parent: worries the process is becoming unsafe and unproductive

starting_positions: Primary position: the conversation is no longer stabilizing through the same repair attempts.
Secondary position: human mediation support may be necessary if the parenting issue is going to be addressed productively.
likely_openings: I still want to resolve this, but I do not think the conversation is holding together.
We keep ending up in the same emotional place instead of actually narrowing the issue.
